facts.text: Palm Coast officially became a city on December 31, 1999, marking its formal incorporation into Flagler County.
Developed by ITT Community Development Corporation in 1969, the original plan called for 48,000 home sites across 42,000 acres.
An extensive water management system was designed to replenish the area’s water table, featuring 46 miles of freshwater canals and 23 miles of saltwater canals.
Florida’s first serious wildland–urban interface fire occurred in Palm Coast in 1985, burning 131 homes and prompting statewide evacuation.
Palm Coast’s parks and trails showcase native Florida beauty, lined with historic oaks, towering pines, and indigenous vegetation.
The Palm Coast Historical Society, founded in 2000, preserves and shares stories from early land grants to modern pioneers.
